request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: US grants license for new nuclear reactor in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (5 events):
- 2026-05-01 [background] Attorney General Bonta Seeks to Halt Trump Administration’s Illegal Greenlight of Oil Transportation in California Pipelines — California AG's action targets oil pipeline approvals, unrelated to NRC combined-license process, so it doesn't affect COL issuance odds.
- 2026-05-01 [background] Attorney General Bonta Seeks to Halt Trump Administration’s Illegal Greenlight of Oil Transportation - May 1, 2026 — California AG action on oil transport is unrelated to NRC combined-license issuance and does not affect COL odds.
- 2026-04-30 [background] Notice of General Order – Ontario Instrument 33-510 Exemptions from Requirements to Submit Certain Personal Information under OSC Rule 33-506 (Commodity Futures Act) Registration Information — Ontario Securities Commission exemptions are unrelated to U.S. NRC combined licenses and have no bearing on COL issuance probability.
- 2026-03-05 [A] Attorney General Bonta Opposes Trump Administration’s Attempt to Exempt New and Experimental Nuclear Reactors from Environmental Review — Multi-state AG opposition to federal exemption of environmental review raises legal and political hurdles, lowering odds and likely delaying an NRC COL.
- 2026-03-05 [A] Attorney General Bonta Opposes Trump Administration’s Attempt to Exempt Nuclear Reactors - March 5, 2026 — California AG's opposition to federal exemptions could block expedited reviews and make an NRC COL by end-2026 less likely.

response.text: Since March 2026, multistate AGs led by California AG Bonta oppose federal efforts to exempt new/experimental reactors from environmental review, creating legal and political headwinds that could delay NRC combined-license approvals this year. Other listed items (Ontario securities rule and CA oil-pipeline case) are unrelated and don’t affect odds. NRC licensing timelines and any court outcomes on the exemptions remain pending.
